topic: Section 4.6 Registry Framing Audit - adversarial review after EXP-397 4-patch integration (false author_* prose corrected, Currently-classified-claims mirror sub-table added)
our_argument_summary: Section 4.6 audits a six-row gap between the dome's machine-readable API layer (claim_index.json / methodology.json / ai_manifest.json - which publishes concessions: a 5-class taxonomy, strict_prospective_confirmed, registry_integrity_notes for WIN-058/062, registry_semantics=display-level-bookkeeping, CVaR exclusion cap) and the visible homepage / predictions.html / scorecard - which still shows 69 Confirmed, 94.5% accuracy, no caveat, no badges. The fix-EXP just landed (EXP-397) corrects three false prose claims about per-WIN author_* fields that do not exist in data/wins.json, and adds a 9-row mirror sub-table of api/current/claim_index.json as of 2026-05-16. Companion EXP-396 patched Row 2 of the gap-audit table and the TLDR to update the count drift from 10/9/1 to 10/12/1 (reflecting that the original 10-vs-9 manifest-vs-wins-grid gap has closed and a new predictions.html-banner-vs-wins-page 12-vs-10 gap has emerged).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section 4.6 doesn't do its own calculation - it aggregates counts the dome publishes (ai_manifest.json prospective_confirmed=10, predictions.html banner=12, claim_index.json claim_class==prospective_confirmed=1) and reports the divergence.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The sub-table's 9 rows are hardcoded from a 2026-05-16 fetch (analyst-side) of api/current/claim_index.json. There is no repo snapshot of that JSON - the audit is not independently reproducible from the repo alone.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The kernel-of-truth subsection grants the API layer is honest; the critique is narrowed to the visible-layer audience. This insulates against the obvious counter ('the author DID acknowledge').
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Section 4.6 still asserts that strict_prospective_confirmed is a current claim_index.json field (gap-audit row 1, kernel-of-truth paragraph), but the schema-drift note in the same section says strict_prospective_confirmed was removed. Three-way inconsistency in one section.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section 4.6 is a structural audit section, not a per-WIN claim. The monitoring axis does not apply at the section level - flagged none rather than guessed.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not applicable - this section is meta-review, not a phenomenon claim.

holes_found.description: Canary subsection still contains the same false-claim pattern EXP-397 was created to fix. The paragraph reads: 'The per-WIN Author's Self-Rating block and this subsection together form a testable monitor ... If he classifies more claims under the 5-class taxonomy, our WIN cards fill in automatically.' Neither sub-claim is true: data/wins.json has zero author_* fields across all 71 WIN entries (verified - grep returns nothing), so there is no per-WIN Author's Self-Rating block and the WIN cards cannot fill in automatically. EXP-397 audited three false prose occurrences (P1 divergence-paragraph, P2 author_claim_class default, P3 footer See-also link) and patched all three, but this fourth occurrence in the Canary subsection - which is structurally the same false claim asserted in framing-of-the-monitor language - was missed. The credibility argument of Section 4.6 ('we credit the dome for API-level rigor; we audit the visible-layer mismatch') depends on our own visible-layer prose not exhibiting the same mismatch.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch the Canary subsection P1 to re-anchor the monitor on the actual artifacts that exist: the Currently-classified-claims sub-table within this section (analyst-refresh-driven mirror) and the gap-audit table's six rows. Suggested replacement: 'The Currently-classified-claims sub-table and the gap-audit table above together form a testable monitor. If the author updates the homepage to reconcile the 10 / 12 / 1 count drift, we show it resolved. If he classifies more claims under the 5-class taxonomy, the sub-table grows on the next analyst refresh. If he adds the CVaR cap to the headline, Row 6 closes.' Remove the false claim that per-WIN cards fill in automatically.

holes_found.description: Internal contradiction on the status of the strict_prospective_confirmed field. The gap-audit table Row 1 cites 'strict_prospective_confirmed = 1 in claim_index.json' as a current-state fact (treating it as a live API field). The Kernel-of-truth subsection later lists 'strict_prospective_confirmed' alongside registry_integrity_notes and the 5-class taxonomy as honest methodology in the API layer (again implying current existence). But the schema-drift note (added by EXP-397, immediately below the new sub-table) states: 'the dome's claim_index.json was restructured ... strict_prospective_confirmed removed.' Three references in one section, two claim the field is live, one claims it was removed. EXP-397's own evidence_collected_this_run confirms the removal (per the 2026-05-16 live fetch). Either the field was removed and Row 1 / Kernel-of-truth need to be reworded to cite claim_class==prospective_confirmed yields-1 as the current source of the '1' figure, or the field is still present and the schema-drift note is wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: Reconcile to one story. The defensible position (matches the live API per analyst evidence): rephrase Row 1 to 'claim_index.json: only 1 entry tagged claim_class==prospective_confirmed (pred-eclipse-tier3)' and rephrase the Kernel-of-truth list to drop strict_prospective_confirmed and substitute claim_class==prospective_confirmed (the live mechanism). Cross-check: this also tightens the EXP-396 patch which already reframes Row 2 as 'claim_index.json strict subset (claim_class==prospective_confirmed) counts 1.' The two rows should use consistent terminology.

holes_found.description: Canary subsection number drift: it still references 'the 10 / 9 / 1 count drift' even though EXP-396 updated the gap-audit Row 2 and the TLDR to the current 10 / 12 / 1 numbers. Same section, two paragraphs apart, two different numbers for the same quantity. This is precisely the cross-surface inconsistency Section 4.6 audits the dome for - and our own section displays it.
holes_found.recommendation: Update the Canary subsection to read '10 / 12 / 1 count drift' matching the TLDR and Row 2. (Combine into the same patch as the major-hole-1 Canary rewrite above.)

holes_found.description: Row 4 of the gap-audit table ('live.html footer: 67 confirmed' vs 'Homepage: 69 confirmed') is flagged as stale by EXP-396 itself, in its OUT-OF-SCOPE follow-up: 'Row 4 of the same table is now factually stale (live.html footer reads 69 confirmed not 67). Recommend new ISS to update or remove Row 4.' EXP-396 noted but did not patch, and EXP-397 also did not patch this row. The audit therefore publishes a 67-vs-69 gap claim that the dome has actually closed. This is publicly verifiable and a defender would screenshot live.html to disprove it.
holes_found.recommendation: File an ISS to update or remove Row 4. Three options: (a) remove Row 4 entirely as resolved; (b) replace with a different concrete API-vs-visible gap (e.g., the methodology.json registry_exclusion_cap=6 row already covers display absence of caveats, so this slot could be repurposed); (c) re-aim Row 4 at status_history.json wins_confirmed=67 (still observed in poller chg-20260505-1109-001 as out-of-sync with the 69 official) - that auto-pipeline-internal-counter mismatch is the same underlying gap minus the visible-layer claim, and arguably more interesting.

holes_found.description: Sub-table reproducibility: the new 'Currently classified claims' table claims to be 'a direct mirror of the dome's api/current/claim_index.json as of 2026-05-16.' No snapshot of that JSON is captured in the repo (no file under raw-text/, data/, or downloads/). Anyone re-running the review cannot verify the 9 entries against a fixed reference - they would have to re-fetch the live API, which may have changed by then. EXP-397 evidence flags that claim_index.json's last_updated metadata still says 2026-04-17 even though the structure has changed under that timestamp - meaning the dome can silently update without bumping the metadata. The TLDR ('as of 2026-05-16') concedes the snapshot date but doesn't pin the artifact.
holes_found.recommendation: Capture a copy of api/current/claim_index.json under data/dome-api-snapshots/claim_index-2026-05-16.json (or similar). Reference it from the sub-table heading: 'mirror of api/current/claim_index.json as captured 2026-05-16 (see snapshot).' This makes the audit independently reproducible the way raw-text/01-home.txt makes our homepage criticisms reproducible. This is a recommendation, not a blocker.

holes_found.description: Sub-table denominator framing: the lead-in text says '9 of 69+ wins total' but the canonical wins count is 71 (data/wins.json). The '69+' hedge conflates the dome's 69-confirmed-headline population with our 71-WIN-review population. Either basis is defensible if stated; '69+' is sloppy.
holes_found.recommendation: Either '9 of 69 confirmed claims' (using the dome's headline population - natural for an audit OF the dome's classification surface) or '9 of 71 wins our review covers' (using our population). Avoid the open-ended +.

holes_found.description: Sub-table column 'registry_integrity_notes flag' shows three rows flagged 'yes (duplicate visible label)' - for win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-058-theta-rule, win-062-tesla-wave-speed. The dome's API actually contains only two registry_integrity_notes (one for WIN-058, one for WIN-062), per chg-20260417-1620-004 and EXP-397 evidence (dome_registry_integrity_notes_count: 2). The mapping is correct - two notes cover three claim_keys because two claim_keys share the WIN-058 visible label - but a reader counting 'yes' cells reads 3 where the API has 2. A footnote would prevent this misread.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a one-line footnote under the sub-table: 'The dome's claim_index.json publishes two registry_integrity_notes - one covering the two WIN-058 claim_keys (shared visible label), one covering WIN-062. Three rows are flagged yes because two claim_keys map to WIN-058.'

stronger_arguments.description: Reflexivity / credibility framing: Section 4.6's core thrust - that the dome's API layer publishes concessions which haven't propagated to its visible-layer headline - is undermined when our own visible-layer prose has four internal inconsistencies in one section (Canary references nonexistent author_* fields; Canary uses 10/9/1 while TLDR and Row 2 use 10/12/1; strict_prospective_confirmed cited as live in Row 1 / Kernel-of-truth while schema-drift note says removed; Row 4 cites a 67 confirmed footer that the dome has updated to 69). A defender quoting any of these back at us asks: 'You hold us to claim/data consistency but your own audit section does not consistency-check itself before publication.' The fix is mechanical - one minor_edit pass on the Canary subsection and one row update - but it is load-bearing for the section's polemical force.

The audit-monitor property would be sharper if it were tied explicitly to the (a) cadence of analyst refresh (when does the sub-table next update?) and (b) the dome's own claim_index.json refresh rhythm (the API's last_updated metadata of 2026-04-17 hasn't moved while content has - that itself is a finding worth surfacing, because it weakens the 'machine-readable layer is more rigorous' credit slightly: a rigorous API layer would update its last_updated timestamp on structural restructure).
sources: EXP-397 evidence: dome_claim_index_last_updated_field: '2026-04-17 (unchanged - but structure has changed under that timestamp)'

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-397 made the right scope-correction call (EXP-397.decision). The original EXP-183 schema extension (five author_* fields on every WIN with a render template) had real audit value but the proposed source fields stopped tracking the dome's API after two of five were retired (EXP-397.evidence_collected_this_run cites: author_strict_prospective_confirmed and author_registry_status no longer have a source). Building a 60-WIN render pipeline on a moving target is exactly the kind of premature schema commitment we criticize the dome for elsewhere - a 40% staleness rate after 28 days (EXP-397.anticipated_objections) is high. The mirror-sub-table approach preserves the audit signal (visible mirror of the author's classified subset) with one update path instead of three (wins.json fields + generate-html.js template + per-WIN CSS).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Scope correction was correct; execution missed four occurrences of the same falsified premise. The Canary subsection (EXP-397.problem_discovered cited three: P1, P2, P3 - the fourth is the Canary block) still asserts that per-WIN cards exist and fill in automatically. EXP-397 explicitly aimed to fix 'the three false prose claims'; the count was wrong - there were four. The kernel of truth (scope-correct rather than implement, leaner audit path) survives this finding; what needs a follow-up is the same sweep applied to the Canary block plus three smaller fixes (Row 1 / Kernel-of-truth strict_prospective_confirmed terminology, Row 4 stale 67-confirmed footer, sub-table 69+ denominator).

text_fingerprint.verdict: Verdict not applicable at section level - this is meta-review prose. Section stands; minor_edit recommended.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-397-plus-EXP-396 double-patch was good triage in principle: EXP-396 reframed the count-drift row to the current 10/12/1 numbers, and EXP-397 took the deferred ISS-1147 schema-extension work and converted it to a scope-correction with a leaner mirror table. The combined effect is that the visible Section 4.6 is substantively more honest than it was a day ago - it no longer claims per-WIN author_* fields exist (in three of four places). The remaining gap is the Canary subsection, which uses the per-WIN-self-rating framing as load-bearing language for the audit-monitor concept ('The per-WIN Author's Self-Rating block and this subsection together form a testable monitor'). That framing is what made the false claim sticky enough to survive - it's not just a description of a field, it's the rationale for why the section is a monitor at all. Re-anchoring the monitor on the actual artifacts that exist (the Currently-classified-claims sub-table, which DOES update on analyst refresh; and the gap-audit table, which DOES update when the dome edits the visible layer) preserves the monitor concept without the false dependency on per-WIN cards. Suggested rewrite is in the major hole #1 recommendation. The strict_prospective_confirmed three-way inconsistency is a smaller fix - pick one terminology (claim_class==prospective_confirmed yields 1) and apply uniformly. The Row 4 stale row was already self-flagged by EXP-396; converting that flag into an ISS or follow-up EXP is process hygiene. Considering the other side: it's defensible to say 'we patched the three places where the false claim was load-bearing for a different argument; the Canary references it in passing as part of the testable-monitor framing, which a charitable reader will read as aspirational rather than as a current-state claim.' That defense is weak - the Canary text is in present tense ('fill in automatically'), not aspirational ('would fill in'), and the section's own polemic against the dome rests on holding present-tense claims to present-tense data. We don't get to rely on charitable readings of our own prose when the audit thesis is that uncharitable readings of the dome's prose are the load-bearing ones. Treating both sides symmetrically, the Canary needs the same correction.

citations_verified: data/wins.json has 71 entries (node -e count, all 71 verdict-tallied: Refuted by Data 11 / Self-Contradicted 20 / Misleading 16 / Std Model Explains 18 / Not Demonstrated 3 / Unfalsifiable 3)
grep author_ on data/wins.json returns 0 matches - confirms no author_* fields on any WIN
monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-004 verifies dome publishes registry_integrity_notes for WIN-058 and WIN-062 in claim_index.json
monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-005 verifies dome publishes registry_semantics=display-level-bookkeeping in ai_manifest.json and agent_rules instructions for AI auditors

EXP-397.evidence_collected_this_run cites analyst 2026-05-16 fetch of api/current/claim_index.json showing 9 classified items and 2 registry_integrity_notes; cites schema restructure (entries -> claims, strict_prospective_confirmed removed, registry_status -> status)
EXP-396 OUT-OF-SCOPE follow-up acknowledges Row 4 of gap-audit-table is stale (live.html footer reads 69 confirmed not 67)
EXP-397 patches P1-P4 confirmed applied to data/sections.json part4.html (find-and-replace anchors no longer match original false-prose strings)
citations_failed: No repo snapshot of api/current/claim_index.json or ai_manifest.json captured - the sub-table content cannot be independently verified from the repo. Analyst evidence is trusted but not independently auditable. Recommend capturing a snapshot under data/dome-api-snapshots/ for reproducibility.
citations_unchecked: methodology.json content (referenced in gap-audit rows 3, 6) - not captured in repo; relying on EXP-183 and EXP-397 narratives
live.html current footer text (referenced in Row 4) - flagged stale by EXP-396 but not re-verified by this curmudgeon pass

out_of_scope_observations_for_decider: EXP-396 OUT-OF-SCOPE follow-up about Row 4 has not yet become an ISS - folding it in here as hole #4. Decider should consider opening an ISS or batching with the Canary fix in a single follow-up EXP.

Sub-table reproducibility (hole #5) is a meta-process improvement - capturing dome API snapshots under data/dome-api-snapshots/ - that applies beyond Section 4.6 (any section that cites live API content). Not blocking; suggest separate proposal.
summary_for_decider: EXP-397 + EXP-396 collectively improved Section 4.6 (three false author_* prose claims removed, count-drift row updated to 10/12/1, mirror sub-table added). The current verdict and analytical thrust of the section stand. Seven follow-on holes remain, all minor_edit class: (1) MAJOR - Canary subsection still contains a fourth occurrence of the same false author_* claim EXP-397 fixed in three places - same patch type, same justification, missed only because the language framed it as 'testable monitor' rather than as a field description; (2) MAJOR - three-way internal inconsistency on strict_prospective_confirmed (Row 1 + Kernel-of-truth treat it as live API; schema-drift note says removed) - pick one terminology and apply uniformly; (3) MODERATE - Canary uses old 10/9/1 numbers where TLDR and Row 2 are 10/12/1; (4) MODERATE - Row 4 (67-vs-69 footer) flagged stale by EXP-396 itself but not patched; (5) MODERATE - sub-table is not independently reproducible from the repo (no snapshot of claim_index.json captured); (6-7) MINOR - denominator framing ('69+') and footnote-able registry_integrity_notes count (3 yes cells from 2 notes). Recommend a small follow-up EXP combining the Canary rewrite + strict_prospective_confirmed terminology cleanup + Row 4 replacement, then a separate snapshot-capture proposal for reproducibility. No verdict change. queue_id 363 was a legitimate priority push - the patches do what they claim; they just stopped one fix short of full consistency.
queue_id_assertion: queue_id set to 363, matches queue item (target_id=part4, pushed_by=decider, class=deep-attack)
